mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signals a strengthening labor market and broader economic recovery, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between employment growth and equity market performance.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive for 3 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold indicates that the labor market is healing, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn supports corporate profits and drives S&P 500 returns higher over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 9.5% and stays below for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is currently 9.7%, a fall below 9.5% would be a significant improvement.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 9.5% and 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of subsequent periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is maintained at very low levels while industrial production shows signs of expansion, it creates a highly supportive environment for corporate investment and growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is currently 0.2%, indicating accommodative policy.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with nascent industrial recovery
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% and INDPRO growth exceeds 2% for 3 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation above a certain threshold signals potential overheating or supply-side pressures, which typically prompts central bank tightening and erodes real corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflationary pressures can lead to monetary tightening.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4.0% for 2 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of subsequent periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust month-over-month growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer demand and spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of economic activity.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 1-month growth exceeds 0.5% for 2 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals a recovery in the residential construction sector, which has significant positive spillover effects across the broader economy, boosting overall economic activity and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is a key economic indicator, especially post-crisis.
failure_condition: If HOUST 3-month moving average exceeds 650 for 3 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of subsequent periods.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low Federal Funds Rate signals an aggressively accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital, encourages borrowing and investment, and supports higher equity valuations over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6 consecutive months, yet the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP is growing and the unemployment rate is simultaneously falling, it signifies a robust and broad-based economic recovery, which typically leads to increased corporate earnings and sustained positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth and falling unemployment are classic signs of economic recovery.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows positive growth and UNRATE declines by more than 0.1pp over 3 months, yet the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period.
